manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: salad, barracuda, spear, boots, egg, toad, panther, jockstrap, wasp.
Your opponent's team words are: tongs, poncho, bagpipe, magazine, handgun, bellybutton, escalator, microscope.
Distractor words are: cranberry, dustpan, longbow, chimpanzee, toothbrush, warthog, burger.
The kill words are: eagle.

    *   Kill Word: eagle.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: salad, barracuda, spear, boots, egg, toad, panther, jockstrap, wasp.
Your opponent's team words are: tongs, poncho, bagpipe, magazine, handgun, bellybutton, escalator, microscope.
Distractor words are: cranberry, dustpan, longbow, chimpanzee, toothbrush, warthog, burger.
The kill words are: eagle.

CLUE: ARMOR
TARGETS: jockstrap, boots, egg, wasp

The word spear was guessed correctly. The word longbow was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: salad, barracuda, boots, egg, toad, panther, jockstrap, wasp.
Remaining words for your opponent are: tongs, poncho, bagpipe, magazine, handgun, bellybutton, escalator, microscope.
Remaining distractor words are: cranberry, dustpan, chimpanzee, toothbrush, warthog, burger.
Remaining kill words are: eagle.

Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: salad, barracuda, spear, boots, egg, toad, panther, jockstrap, wasp.
Your opponent's team words are: tongs, poncho, bagpipe, magazine, handgun, bellybutton, escalator, microscope.
Distractor words are: cranberry, dustpan, longbow, chimpanzee, toothbrush, warthog, burger.
The kill words are: eagle.

CLUE: ARMOR
TARGETS: jockstrap, boots, egg, wasp

The word spear was guessed correctly. The word longbow was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: salad, barracuda, boots, egg, toad, panther, jockstrap, wasp.

Remaining distractor words are: cranberry, dustpan, chimpanzee, toothbrush, warthog, burger.
Remaining kill words are: eagle.

CLUE: RAW

Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Goal: Provide a single-word clue and a list of target words from my remaining team words.
    *   Format:
        CLUE: <WORD>
        TARGETS: <TARGETS>
    *   Constraints: No extra text, short answers, clue must be semantically related, cannot be one of the words or contain parts of them.
    *   Context: Codenames game simulation.
    *   Remaining Team Words: barracuda, boots, panther, jockstrap.
    *   Remaining Opponent Words: tongs, poncho, bagpipe, magazine, bellybutton, escalator.
    *   Remaining Distractor Words: cranberry, dustpan, chimpanzee, toothbrush, warthog.
    *   Remaining Kill Word: eagle.

2.  **Evaluate Remaining Team Words:**
    *   barracuda (fish, predator, sharp teeth, ocean)
    *   boots (footwear, military, protection, leather)
    *   panther (cat, predator, black, animal, sports team)
    *   jockstrap (sports, protection, underwear, athletic)

3.  **Find Associations:**
    *   *barracuda* & *panther*: Both are predators/animals. "PREDATOR", "ANIMAL", "HUNTER".
        *   Risk: "ANIMAL" might attract *chimpanzee*, *warthog* (distractors), *eagle* (kill). "PREDATOR" might attract *eagle* (kill).
    *   *boots* & *jockstrap*: Both involve protection or sports gear. "GEAR", "PROTECTION", "SPORTS".
        *   Risk: "SPORTS" might attract nothing specific from opponents, but "GEAR" is vague. "PROTECTION" is okay.
    *   *barracuda* & *boots*: Hard to link.
    *   *panther* & *boots*: "PUMA" (brand), but clue cannot contain parts of words (Puma contains none, but it's a brand association). "CAT" (boots? Puss in Boots).
    *   *jockstrap* & *panther*: Sports team (Florida Panthers wear jockstraps? Weak).
    *   *barracuda* & *jockstrap*: Both start with 'b'? No. Both sound aggressive? No.

        *   Kill check: "eagle" (car? Chrysler Eagle?).

    *   *boots* & *jockstrap*: "ATHLETIC", "UNDERWEAR" (boots no), "GEAR".
    *   *barracuda* & *panther*: "CAT" (Panther is a cat, Barracuda is not). "FISH" (Barracuda is fish, Panther is not).
    *   *panther* & *barracuda*: Both are aggressive? "ATTACK"?
    *   *boots* & *jockstrap*: "CUP" (Jockstrap holds a cup, boots cover... no). "SPORT".
    *   *barracuda* & *boots*: "SOLE" (fish sole, boot sole). "Barracuda" doesn't have a sole association strongly.
    *   *panther* & *jockstrap*: "FLORIDA" (Florida Panthers, Florida Gators? No).
    *   *boots* & *panther*: "PUSS" (Puss in Boots, Pussycat/Panther). Risky.
    *   *barracuda* & *panther*: Both are mascots? "MASCOT".
    *   *boots* & *jockstrap*: "LOCKER" (Locker room).
    *   *barracuda*, *panther*, *boots*, *jockstrap*: Is there a link for all 4?
        *   "GEAR"? (Fishing gear, Sports gear, Boots gear).
        *   "STRAP"? (Jockstrap, Boot strap). Barracuda? Panther? No.
        *   "BLACK"? (Black panther, Black boots, Barracuda car black? Jockstrap usually black/white).
        *   "LEATHER"? (Boots, Jockstrap, Panther skin, Barracuda skin). Risky (animal cruelty connotation, also distractor warthog/chimp).
        *   "SKIN"? (Boots leather skin, Panther skin, Barracuda skin, Jockstrap fabric skin?).
        *   "PROTECTION"? (Boots protect feet, Jockstrap protects groin, Panther protects territory?, Barracuda protects?). Weak for animals.
        *   "SPORTS"? (Jockstrap yes, Boots sometimes (soccer/cleats), Panther (team), Barracuda (team?)).
        *   "TEAM"? (Panthers, Barracudas (sports teams exist), Boots (no), Jockstrap (no)).

    *   Let's look at pairs/triplets.
    *   *boots* & *jockstrap*: "CUP" (Jockstrap cup, Boot cup? No). "ATHLETIC".
    *   *barracuda* & *panther*: "PREDATOR". (Avoid eagle).
    *   *barracuda* & *panther*: "CAT" (Panther yes, Barracuda no).
    *   *barracuda* & *panther*: "FISH" (Barracuda yes, Panther no).
    *   *boots* & *jockstrap*: "UNDERWEAR" (Jockstrap yes, Boots no).
    *   *boots* & *jockstrap*: "GEAR".
    *   *panther* & *boots*: "PUMA". (Brand of boots, synonym for panther). This is a strong link for 2 words.

Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

TARGETS: <TARGETS>
